mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals rising household income and lower near-term recession risk, so stronger PAYEMS momentum produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS 3-month growth has Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window or if positive payroll momentum is followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment signals accelerating slack and earnings downgrades; when UNRATE increases sharply, investors reprice recession risk and S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: Falling housing starts reduce construction activity, durable-goods demand, and bank credit quality; when HOUST breaks below its prior 12-month low, S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month minimum
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns after HOUST downside breakouts are positive in more than 60% of triggered cases or if the average triggered return is not statistically below zero at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates with rising inflation pressure increase discount rates and reduce equity duration valuations; when FEDFUNDS is elevated while CPIAUCSL momentum is positive, S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS and CPI trigger produces nonnegative average 20-day forward returns or if its downside hit rate is below 50% in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive industrial production momentum signals improving operating leverage and revenue expectations for cyclical firms; rising INDPRO over the prior 3 months predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO 3-month momentum has Rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window or if positive INDPRO momentum underperforms negative momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales contraction directly signals weakening household demand and margin pressure; when RSAFS falls for two consecutive months, S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 5 trading days as growth expectations reset.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly change < 0 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if two-month RSAFS declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not below unconditional 5-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth is positive and unemployment is not rising, aggregate demand is expanding without visible labor stress; this combination supports earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter change > 0 and UNRATE 3-month change <= 0.1pp
failure_condition: Reject if the joint growth-and-labor trigger has average 60-day forward returns below the unconditional average or if positive returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing that lowers discount rates and supports risk appetite; when FEDFUNDS falls over the prior 3 months, S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle outside acute credit panic
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if easing-period mean returns are not above unconditional mean returns.
